articles.title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
articles.description:
Two state elections passed with no oversight, advocates said, while Secretary William Galvin's office blamed "technical issues."

The post After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Secretary of Commonwealth William Galvin will produce a report on voting in jail by March 2026.
Two state elections occurred without oversight due to alleged technical issues in Galvin's office.
Advocates argue this report is crucial for ensuring voting rights for incarcerated individuals in Massachusetts.

articles.title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
articles.description:
“I love to run the ball ... especially when the other team knows we're running the ball."

The post New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam compares rookie Drake Maye to MVP Josh Allen.
Gilliam emphasizes his passion for running plays, enhancing the Patriots' offensive strategy.
Boston fans can expect a more aggressive running game this season.

articles.title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
articles.description:
Gonzalez is expected to miss at least the first two months of the 2026 season.

The post Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez underwent shoulder surgery on March 12, 2026.
Gonzalez is expected to miss at least the first two months of the 2026 season.
Boston signed a lefty reliever to bolster the team during Gonzalez's recovery.

articles.title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
articles.description:
“This is not only a tragedy for the town, for the school, this is just a very difficult case because of the randomness of this violence.”

The post High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: High school student allegedly killed a Danvers woman in her home on March 12.
District Attorney describes the attack as a random act of violence affecting the community.
Local schools and residents are grappling with the shock of this tragic incident.
